blocks.content: She was standing twenty feet away watching Eli set up the next round, and she turned to check on them the way she had been doing all night — careful, not wanting to intrude — and she saw Kit's arms around Daisy, and the way Daisy was settled into him, and she knew immediately. She didn't need to see the moment itself. The aftermath said everything.
She pressed her hand over her mouth. Breathed through her nose.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: Yes. Yes I'm great. Priya. Look at them.
Priya looked. Her expression did the thing it always did when she'd been let in on something that mattered.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: I have been waiting for this since we were fourteen years old.

blocks.speaker: Priya
blocks.content: How long have they known each other.

blocks.speaker: Savannah
blocks.content: Since they were eight. They've been in love with each other almost that long.
Eli came back from the launch point brushing his hands on his jeans, looking satisfied. He looked toward Kit and Daisy. He went completely still.

Eli Turner — who had planned an entire Fourth of July gathering around this specific outcome, who had measured lantern heights and sourced a cooler that was unreasonably large and checked the weather through his girlfriend — just nodded. Slow. Like a man at peace with himself and the world.
